description_long: This object is a standard Western-style toilet with a prominent rectangular cistern mounted at the back and a rounded bowl at the front. The toilet features a raised seat with an open center, and the lid is shown in an upright position. The flush lever is visible on the left side of the cistern, and a water inlet valve is attached to the lower left rear. The base is broad and flat, designed for stable floor mounting, and the overall form is smooth with subtle angular transitions between the tank, bowl, and base. The surface appears to be made of glazed ceramic, giving it a clean, slightly glossy finish. There are no visible accessibility features such as grab bars or raised seats.
description: A floor-mounted, Western-style toilet with a rectangular cistern, rounded bowl, and visible flush lever. The surface is smooth and slightly glossy, typical of glazed ceramic.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open seat, upright lid, and the full width of the cistern.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the flush lever, water inlet valve, and the profile of the bowl and base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the cistern and the base, with the water inlet valve visible.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the side profile of the cistern, seat, and base, with the flush lever less visible.
